article_items.title: Meta's new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Meta announced Monday that it's rolling out a wave of new AI features on Facebook, the latest sign of the company's effort to catch up in the AI race and keep users more engaged on the platform.
article_items.excerpt: Meta announced Monday that it's rolling out a wave of new AI features on Facebook, the latest sign of the company's effort to catch up in the AI race and keep users more engaged on the platform.
article_items.summary: Alongside these feature rollouts, the company recently launched global subscription plans for Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp - starting at $3.99 a month - that unlock additional features, with more AI-related subscription tiers reportedly on the way. More from TechCrunch Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms As Meta tries to catch up in the AI race and boost engagement with its AI bot, the company announced Monday that it’s.

This follows Meta’s quiet launch last month of Forum , a Reddit-style app that includes its own AI “Ask” tab, letting users pose questions and get answers pulled from discussions happening across Facebook Groups.
Taken together, the flurry of releases points to a broader strategy: Meta wants Facebook’s AI tools to make the platform stickier and more useful, while also diversifying how it makes money.

Most recently, earlier this month, Facebook launched an AI assistant for creators that offers personalized suggestions - including the best times to post and summaries of what audiences are saying in the comments -.
Another new feature is the AI-powered photo presets, allowing users to change up their look with different clothes, hairstyles, and accessories.

Sports fans, for instance, can virtually wear their favorite team jerseys just by tapping the “AI Edit” icon in Stories and choose “Wear It,” or go directly to their profile picture and select “Restyle profile picture.

Because the AI is summarizing content from everyday users rather than vetted sources, there’s a real risk of outdated or misleading information slipping through, a concern that’s already been raised about Google’s own.
In February, the company introduced animated profile pictures that bring still photos to life - adding a wave, or placing a virtual party hat on someone’s head.
The headline update is “AI Mode,” a new way to search Facebook that uses Meta AI to surface answers pulled from public posts across the platform, including Groups and Reels.

article_items.title: Introducing Gemma 4 models on Amazon Bedrock | Amazon Web Services
article_items.description: Today, we are announcing the availability of the Gemma 4 family on Amazon Bedrock. Built by Google DeepMind and released under the Apache 2.0 license, Gemma 4 is a family of open-weight models designed with a focus on intelligence-per-parameter across a broad range of deployment scenarios. The family includes three instruction-tuned variants: Gemma 4 31B, Gemma 4 26B-A4B, and Gemma 4 E2B. These cover dense and mixture-of-experts (MoE) architectures, where only a fraction of the model’s parameters activate per request. The variants offer built-in reasoning, native function calling, and multimodal input across text and image.
article_items.excerpt: Today, we are announcing the availability of the Gemma 4 family on Amazon Bedrock. These cover dense and mixture-of-experts (MoE) architectures, where only a fraction of the model’s parameters activate per request. The variants offer built-in reasoning, native function calling, and multimodal input across text and image.

article_items.title: AI Agent Failure Detection and Root Cause Analysis with Strands Evals | Amazon Web Services
article_items.description: In this post, we walk you through calling the detector functions to diagnose real agent failures. You learn how to interpret their structured output: categorized failures with confidence scores, causal chains linking root causes to downstream symptoms, and fix recommendations specifying whether a change belongs in your system prompt or tool definitions. You also learn how to integrate detection into your evaluation pipeline for automated diagnosis on every test run.
article_items.excerpt: In this post, we walk you through calling the detector functions to diagnose real agent failures. You learn how to interpret their structured output: categorized failures with confidence scores, causal chains linking root causes to downstream symptoms, and fix recommendations specifying whether a change belongs in your system prompt or tool definitions. You also learn how to integrate detection into your evaluation pipeline for automated diagnosis on every test run.

Anthropic executives met with administration officials over the weekend after the government directed generative AI vendor to suspend access to some of its newest AI models.

A June 12 statement on Anthropic's website revealed that the Trump administration, citing national security authorities, had issued an export control directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign.
The statement said the government had not provided specific details about its concern.
The response to the move has been mixed, with some industry observers bemused.
